name: Shrimp and Crab Enchiladas in Chipotle Cream Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 chipotle peppers in adobo, to taste
1/4 teaspoon toasted cumin seed (or cumin powder)
1/2 cup lowfat sour cream, thinned with
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine 4 cups water, bay leaves, and salt. Bring to a boil.
2. Add shrimp and cook for 2 minutes. Drain, chop finely, and mix with crab meat. Set aside.
3. Prepare the Crema de Chipotle: Combine tomatoes, tomatillos, and ancho chiles in a large saucepan with water. Simmer for 5 minutes, then drain. Remove skins from garlic and onion slice; blend with tomatoes, tomatillos, ancho chiles, chipotle peppers, cumin, salt, and 1 cup reserved cooking water. Puree and strain into a bowl.
4. Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the remaining onion slice, blacken, and discard. Add the Crema de Chipotle to the pan and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Reduce heat to low and stir in sour cream. Keep warm over low heat.
5. Preheat oven to 375F. Soften tortillas (see TIP below) and roll into enchiladas, filling each with about 3 tablespoons shrimp and crab mixture. Bake for 8-10 minutes until cooked but not crisped. Serve three per person, covering each portion with plenty of sauce.
TIP: To soften tortillas, run them under running water, shake off excess, and place in a hot non-stick frying pan over medium heat. Heat for about 15 seconds on one side, then flip and heat the other side. Roll immediately. Alternatively, wrap 5-10 tortillas in plastic bag with 1/4 teaspoon water and microwave for 45-60 seconds.
